absorptivity.sourceName: Absorptivity of Copper Alloys in the Infrared for Laser Processing

laserPower.description: Optimal average power for metal oxide removal

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal metal absorption

spotSize.description: Beam diameter for precise industrial cleaning; smaller for fine details on Aluminum Bronze parts, larger for broad surfaces. 200 μm recommended for uniform energy distribution and efficiency. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems, optimized for alloy surface prep.

frequency.description: Typical for pulsed fiber lasers in cleaning; higher rates speed up process but risk heat accumulation in reflective Aluminum Bronze. 50 kHz balances speed and thermal management. Trade-off: >80 kHz may require cooling. Source: Academic literature in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2021) on repetition rate effects.

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond regime dominant for cost-effective industrial cleaning; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) reduce HAZ but are less common for Aluminum Bronze due to high reflectivity. 50 ns recommended for minimal thermal effects on conductive alloys. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) comparing pulse regimes for Cu-Al alloys.

scanSpeed.description: Effective speeds for 1064 nm cleaning; lower for heavy contamination (e.g., oxide layers on Aluminum Bronze), higher for light rust/oil. 1500 mm/s recommended for optimal contaminant removal without excessive heat buildup. Source: Industry standards from Laserax manuals and Journal of Laser Applications (2020) case studies on bronze alloys.

passCount.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy layers like marine corrosion on Aluminum Bronze. 2 passes recommended for complete removal with minimal risk. Source: Industry practice in case studies from Surface and Coatings Technology (2022) on bronze cleaning applications.

overlapRatio.description: Ensures uniform coverage without gaps or over-ablation; 60% typical for Aluminum Bronze to account for reflectivity variations. Range adjusted for contamination thickness. Source: Process optimization studies in Optics & Laser Technology (2020) on scan strategies for metals.
